name: Bihar
stopoverNote: Fly into Patna or seasonal Gaya for the Buddhist circuit; the coherent route is Patna → Nalanda → Rajgir → Bodh Gaya, with Nalanda and Rajgir close enough to combine on one transfer day.

diet.vegetarian: Bihar is very vegetarian-friendly: litti chokha, sattu paratha, dal pitha, khichdi, chana ghugni, thekua and khaja are easy to find. Bodh Gaya is especially veg-friendly because of its Buddhist pilgrimage scene, with monastery kitchens and pure-veg cafés.
diet.vegan: Traditional chokha and sattu drinks can be vegan, but ghee, curd and milk sweets are common. Ask for litti, dal or khichdi without ghee and skip dahi; Bodh Gaya is the easiest stop for fully plant-based meals.
phrases: Bina ghee ka banana = please make it without ghee
Doodh/dahi nahi chahiye = I do not want milk or curd
Sattu aur litti chokha milega? = do you have sattu and litti chokha?

cities.name: Patna
cities.notes: Bihar's ancient capital, once Pataliputra of the Mauryan Empire, with Sikh pilgrimage at Takht Sri Patna Sahib, the Bihar Museum and Ganga riverfront culture.

cities.name: Nalanda
cities.notes: UNESCO-listed remains of the ancient residential university, with brick monasteries, temples, lecture halls and a compact archaeological museum.

cities.name: Rajgir
cities.notes: Ancient Magadha capital where Buddha and Mahavira taught, known for Vishwa Shanti Stupa, Venuvan, Brahma Kund hot springs and cyclopean walls.

cities.name: Bodh Gaya
cities.notes: The holiest Buddhist site, centred on the UNESCO Mahabodhi Temple and Bodhi Tree where Siddhartha Gautama attained enlightenment.

itinerary.sim: Any Indian prepaid SIM (Jio or Airtel) works well in Patna, Nalanda, Rajgir and Bodh Gaya; buy at the airport or an authorised store with photo ID. Coverage is strongest in Patna and Bodh Gaya, with occasional weak patches on rural transfer roads.

Patna.name: Patna
Patna.note: Two days gives Patna proper context: one for the museum, Sikh heritage and Ganga ghats, one for Pataliputra archaeology and local food.

days.note: Patna sights are spread out but manageable by auto or app-cab; keep Bihar Museum as the anchor and end on the Ganga riverfront.

activities.name: Bihar Museum — Didarganj Yakshi and state heritage galleries
activities.tip: Closed Mondays; start near opening time so the museum does not squeeze the day.

activities.name: Takht Sri Patna Sahib — birthplace of Guru Gobind Singh
activities.tip: Cover your head, remove shoes and leave time for the langar if it is running.

activities.name: Golghar — climb the granary for Ganga views
activities.tip: Go early for clearer light and fewer school groups.

activities.name: Ganga Riverfront at Gandhi Ghat or Patna Ghat
activities.tip: Sunset is best; boat rides and snacks are paid locally.

meals: Litti chokha and sattu paratha near the riverfront
Thekua or khaja from a local sweet shop

days.note: A lighter day suits archaeology lovers; Kumhrar and the old museum/library area are short cab hops inside the city.

activities.name: Kumhrar Archaeological Park — Pataliputra ruins
activities.tip: A local guide helps explain the 80-pillar hall remains.

activities.name: Khuda Bakhsh Oriental Library
activities.tip: Best for manuscript and history enthusiasts; check opening days before going.

activities.name: Boring Road / Rajendra Nagar food trail
activities.tip: Look for sattu, dal pitha, chana ghugni and litti chokha.

meals: Dal pitha or chana ghugni lunch
Litti chokha dinner without ghee if vegan

Nalanda.name: Nalanda
Nalanda.note: Nalanda works as a focused half-to-full day between Patna and Rajgir; the ruins and museum are the essential pair.

days.note: The ruins, museum and nearby memorial fit cleanly before continuing 14 km to Rajgir the same day.

activities.name: Nalanda University Ruins (ASI UNESCO Site)
activities.tip: Hire a licensed guide at the gate if available; the site rewards context.

activities.name: Nalanda Archaeological Museum
activities.tip: Closed Fridays; compact but rich in Buddhist bronzes and sculpture.

hotels.name: Simple Nalanda guesthouse if staying local

meals: Simple vegetarian thali near the ruins
Sattu drink or seasonal fruit before the Rajgir transfer

Rajgir.name: Rajgir
Rajgir.note: One full day covers the ropeway, Venuvan, hot springs and key ruins; add a relaxed second day only for hiking or lake time.

days.theme: Hilltop stupa, hot springs & Magadhan ruins
days.note: Rajgir's main sights are close, but the ropeway queues build; go early, then use autos for hot springs and ruins.

activities.name: Vishwa Shanti Stupa + Rajgir Ropeway
activities.tip: Check BSTDC status and arrive for the first ropeway when possible.

activities.name: Venuvan (Bamboo Grove)
activities.tip: A peaceful early-morning stop tied to Buddhist monastic history.

activities.name: Brahma Kund Hot Springs
activities.tip: Bring a change of clothes if bathing; water can be very hot.

meals: Litti chokha with chokha made in mustard oil
Tea and snacks near the ropeway base

days.theme: Slow Rajgir morning before Bodh Gaya
days.note: Use this optional buffer if you arrived late from Nalanda; the 70 km transfer to Bodh Gaya is still feasible after lunch.

activities.name: Brahma Kund early bath or temple circuit revisit
Short Ratnagiri or local hill walk
activities.tip: Avoid in summer heat and during heavy monsoon.

meals: Roadside vegetarian dhaba on the Bodh Gaya road

Bodh Gaya.name: Bodh Gaya
Bodh Gaya.note: Two days is the minimum to absorb Mahabodhi, the international monasteries and the Gaya/Dungeswari side trip without rushing.

days.note: Bodh Gaya is compact and walkable; spend unhurried time at Mahabodhi and return around dusk for chants.

activities.name: Mahabodhi Temple Complex + Bodhi Tree (UNESCO)
activities.tip: Shoes stay outside; cover shoulders and knees and return for evening prayer if possible.

activities.name: International monastery circuit walk
activities.tip: Thai, Tibetan, Japanese, Bhutanese and other monasteries sit within a compact circuit.

meals: Thai Monastery vegetarian kitchen when open
Sattu or simple thali near the temple lanes

days.note: Gaya is 13 km away and Dungeswari is a manageable side trip; keep Mahabodhi sunrise as the emotional bookend.

activities.name: Vishnupad Temple and Phalgu River ghats, Gaya
activities.tip: Inner sanctum access is restricted to Hindus; ghats and exterior areas are atmospheric for all visitors.

activities.name: Dungeswari (Mahakala) Caves
activities.tip: Expect a short climb and possible donation requests.

activities.name: Bodh Gaya market for prayer flags and handicrafts

meals: GoVeg Project or another plant-based Bodh Gaya café
Litti chokha without ghee if vegan

connections.method: Private car/taxi or state bus via Biharsharif
Auto-rickshaw, shared tempo, or private taxi
Private taxi/car hire or shared cab to Gaya plus auto

extras: Classic Buddhist circuit timing: Patna (2d) → Nalanda (1d) → Rajgir (1d) → Bodh Gaya (2d), with Nalanda and Rajgir close enough to share a transfer day if short on time.
Book Bodh Gaya stays early for November–February pilgrimage season and especially around major Buddhist teachings or delegations.
Use Gaya Airport for a Buddhist-circuit-first trip when seasonal flights align; otherwise Patna is the most reliable domestic gateway.
Quoted fees are only those verified in the Bihar research report; other attraction costs are marked as varies and should be reconfirmed locally.
